title: It Was a Pretty Dang Epic Day on the CANARY ISLANDS
text: This is the strange little island of La Graciosa. Canary Islands, Espana. And there is Lanzarote. Hey. guys, how's it going? Good afternoon. Today is Monday, May 4th, 2026. Currently 70° Fahrenheit, that is 21° C here on lovely Lanzarote Island in the Canary Islands. Another amazing, perfect, sunny, warmish day with a breeze. So, I am at the uh northern end of the island near the village of Orzola. I drove here with my rental car yesterday and attempted to take the ferry over to La Graciosa Island. I'm not sure if that is La Graciosa there or part of Lanzarote. I think it must be La Graciosa because Orzola is at the northern tip of Lanzarote Island. So, uh I got here at 1:50 in the afternoon yesterday. I had looked online at the uh ferry schedules, but had just taken a quick snapshot and had seen that the ferries depart every hour or hour and a half or so. And so, I thought, "Well, I'll just get up here and then buy a ticket for the next one and then if it's an hour later, then explore here or whatever." But, when I arrived, I discovered that there is a gap between 1:30 is when one of the ferries departs and then the next one isn't until 4:00 in the afternoon. So, there's like a siesta gap, basically, for uh the middle of the afternoon with no ferries going there or coming back. And I didn't want to wait around until 4:00 and didn't want to get that late of a start anyways. And so I changed plans and instead drove up there where I got an absolutely epic view of the island of La Graciosa. And it was in large part because of getting that view that I decided, yes, it is worth coming back here and attempting again. Even though I am missing out on seeing other parts of Lanzarote island because this is my last day with the rental car. Well, I return it tomorrow at 1:00 at Playa Blanca, which is at the far southern tip of the island. There is much more of the island to see, the entire southern part, which I am basically not going to get to see because I'm not going to get going early enough tomorrow to be able to see much before I drop the car at 1:00 and then take a ferry over to Fuerteventura island and then I will be there for the next three days. So uh in the southern part of the island there are these national parks like volcanic national parks and partly I was fine with skipping them because I can imagine what they're going to be like. I'm sure they're going to be kind of like this. Volcanic rocks, lots of barren hills. I've been to lots of volcanic types of landscapes in the past and uh I think I will be fine with missing that one, but I will be happy to come back another time and explore more of Lanzarote again sometime because I really, really like this island. I was here for 5 days, but uh I'd say it's definitely worth at least a week. And I just really like the vibe, the atmosphere. It's quite slow-paced, little traffic. There are certainly spots that are very touristy. But uh overall, it is quite mellow, and it has lots of these really cute little white villages like Orzola there. And so uh I wanted to uh start off by showing this beach that I passed on the way into the town. So, I've already been there, bought my ferry tickets going and coming back, so it should be a sure thing. Hopefully, fingers crossed, to get over to La Graciosa today. But uh let's take a quick look at this beach. By the looks of it, it is definitely not my preferred beach, being very shallow water, kind of small, and rather crowded. So, not going to uh take a swim here today. And so, you can see very clear water, but very, very shallow. A really unique setting though amongst all these black volcanic rocks. All right, it is time for me to uh get back in the rental car and get over to Orzola and catch the boat. And so, here we are coming into Orzola. Yesterday, I went left up ahead to Mirador del Rio and Haria. Well worth visiting. Mirador del Rio is where I got the view of La Graciosa, and it was fantastic. Here's the rental car, 36 euros or $42 US per day. Pretty good deal. The ferry departs in 15 minutes, so it should be sitting there waiting for us. And you can get around the island by bus. I took one from where I'm standing at Costa Teguise to Guise into Arrecife, the capital and largest town. They run every 20 minutes, so that's great. It was only 1 euro 40, so good deal. Maybe 5 euros or something to get from there up here. But uh at 36 euros for a rental car, that is absolutely the way to go. So, speaking of Arrecife in my video there, then I showed that it was a ghost town basically. Just really really quiet. And so people informed me in the comments section that that was on Friday, May 1st, which is May Day. Which is Workers' Day basically. And so a lot of businesses shut down for that reason on May Day. And then combined with it isn't really the full tourist season yet. Although the Canary Islands are uh visited by Europeans especially and others of course, but uh year round because it is so pleasant here being as far south as we are. And so you can come here in January, which is exactly what I did the first time 9 years ago. And it is still very pleasant weather. A little bit cooler, but not much. But uh it will probably get more busy going into the summer tourist season. And then you figure the war with Iran and gas prices and everything, then that is probably affecting tourism a bit.
